Description: Apply PF-IMP-671 default-quiet pattern (demote INFO/SUCCESS narration to Write-Verbose, emit one always-visible summary line) to sibling automation scripts: Update-ProcessImprovement.ps1, New-ProcessImprovement.ps1, and any other Common-ScriptHelpers consumers with the same Write-Log idiom. PF-IMP-671 proved the pattern on Update-TechDebt.ps1; sibling scripts still emit ~9 verbose lines per invocation (5-6 invocations per PF-TSK-009 session = ~54 lines).
Notes: Recurrence: 3+ forms (PF-FEE-1079, 1083, 1086 conciseness-3 ratings on Update-ProcessImprovement.ps1). Same recipe as PF-IMP-671: demote INFO/SUCCESS to Write-Verbose, silence $VerbosePreference around helper-module import. -Verbose flag restores play-by-play.

Description: Add explicit root-cause-vs-symptom prompt to PF-TSK-009 Step 2 (Verify the problem) or Step 3 (evaluation table). Currently the Minimum Viability criterion partially catches this but doesn't distinguish 'smallest symptom-level change' from 'smallest root-cause change'. Suggested wording: 'Does the IMP's proposed fix target the underlying defect, or route around it via a flag/option/escape hatch? If the latter, what is the underlying defect, and is fixing it in scope?'
Notes: Recurrence: 3+ forms (PF-FEE-1061 root-cause regex restructure on IMP-629; PF-FEE-1073 band-aid framing on IMP-667; PF-FEE-1079 -Quiet vs default-quiet on IMP-671). Each required human pushback to redirect from symptom-level to root-cause fix; codifying the prompt would surface the distinction at the agent's Step 6 proposal rather than relying on human catch.

Description: Add archive-concurrency safeguard to PF-TSK-010 Step 14. Same-date Tools Review sessions both write to tools-review-YYYYMMDD/processed-forms/, causing collision. Either suffix archive folder with session number/timestamp, OR explicitly call out that combined archives are expected and verification check should be 'all my forms are in archive AND count of MY task groups matches my analysis', not 'total archive count matches my count'.
Notes: Recurrence: 2 forms (PF-FEE-1055, 1056) both reporting same incident from opposite sides. ~10-15 min recovery work per session. PF-FEE-1055 even reports a near-miss where one session's restore would have erased another session's correct archive. Real harm with concrete fix path.

Description: Trim verbose post-creation banner in New-FeedbackForm.ps1 (broaden PF-IMP-584's New-IntegrationNarrative.ps1 fix). Triple-banner 'CRITICAL: TEMPLATE CREATED - EXTENSIVE CUSTOMIZATION REQUIRED' (~10 lines) buries real warnings. PF-IMP-584 fixed only New-IntegrationNarrative.ps1; the same pattern persists in New-FeedbackForm.ps1, New-Guide.ps1, and likely other New-* scripts via shared document-creation-script-template.ps1.
Notes: Recurrence: 5+ forms (PF-FEE-986, 1063, 1066, 1080, 1084, 1085 conciseness 3-4 ratings on New-FeedbackForm.ps1). Per memory 'observe before broadening' rule, deferred at first occurrence — now sufficient evidence for cross-script sweep. Consider fixing the shared document-creation-script-template.ps1 once.

Description: Add compressed Step 13 format to PF-TSK-009 for low-risk single-change IMPs. When Step 11 sweep is clean AND change matches Step 6 plan (no deviation), allow short Step 13 ('Risk class confirmed; no deviation from Step 6 plan; approve / revise / reject?') instead of restating the diff. Mirrors Step 8's existing skip clause for trivial cases.
Notes: Recurrence: 3+ forms (PF-FEE-1064, 1081, 1086 all flag Step 13 redundancy for low-risk doc-only IMPs). Step 8 already has equivalent skip clause; Step 13 doesn't. Surfaced post-PF-IMP-689 v2.5 reframe — the conciseness rule applies at Step 6 but not Step 13.

Description: Surface New-ProcessImprovement.ps1 -Description 500-char limit prominently in -? help output. Currently buried inside .PARAMETER Description; agents discover it only on validation failure. Mirror PF-IMP-637 fix for New-BugReport.ps1: surface the cap in synopsis or .NOTES top-level bullet.
Notes: Recurrence: 3+ forms (PF-FEE-1062, 1071, 1082 each report retry due to Description-too-long). PF-IMP-659 already moved limit to .NOTES bullet, but `-File path.ps1 -?` only renders SYNOPSIS+SYNTAX+DESCRIPTION (per PF-FEE-1060) — .PARAMETER and .NOTES aren't visible at default help. Move to .DESCRIPTION.

Description: Address feedback_db.py duplicate/deviation tool entries. Several tools have two registered IDs (e.g., Update-BugStatus vs Update-BugStatus.ps1, New-RefactoringPlan vs New-RefactoringPlan.ps1, test-audit-task.md vs PF-TSK-030). PF-TSK-009 Step 12's canonical convention (PF-TSK-NNN for tasks, filename for everything else) resolves at log time, but historical drift remains. Add list-tools warning when duplicate-feeling IDs exist OR add rename-tool / merge-tools subcommand.
Notes: Recurrence: 4+ forms (PF-FEE-1059, 1066, 1067, 1068, 1083). Convention-doc IMP-623 closed the cause; consolidating the historical drift is the remaining work. Trend analysis is split across the duplicate IDs — visible misleading signal.

Description: Add common-stale-description-sites checklist to PF-TSK-009 Step 11 (linked-document housekeeping). Non-exhaustive list of sites where descriptions can drift even when links are intact: script .PARAMETER/.DESCRIPTION blocks, PF-documentation-map.md script index lines, templates/README.md descriptions, README script tables. Reduces cost of doing Step 11 thoroughly the first time.
Notes: PF-FEE-1076 surfaced this directly (caught Update-TechDebt PF-documentation-map description on third pass; missed templates/README.md descriptions). LinkWatcher catches link drift but not description drift. Single occurrence but high-value preventive checklist.

Description: Add explicit sub-step to PF-TSK-009 Step 2 (Verify the problem): verify that artifact IDs and file paths in the IMP description resolve to real artifacts (PF-id-registry.json, PD-id-registry.json, file existence). If a reference doesn't resolve, treat it as a labeling error in the IMP — locate the actual artifact via search before evaluating, do not reject the IMP solely because a referenced ID is wrong.
Notes: PF-FEE-1081: IMP-678 referenced 'PF-TSK-048' (never registered; actual was PF-TSK-026); IMP-668 said 'L8' but rationale belonged at L3/L7. Two of three IMPs in that session contained labeling errors. Single occurrence but cheap to add.

Description: Add `list` subcommand to feedback_db.py to enumerate registered test_ids and known tool_doc_ids. Currently agents discover known IDs via `export | head` or `list-tools --filter X`. A direct `list` (or `list-test-ids`) subcommand reduces friction when picking sub-IDs for multi-metric tests.
Notes: PF-FEE-1058: had to use `export | head` to discover the multi-metric sub-ID convention (PH-007-net, PH-007-op-delta) for performance baseline capture.

Description: Add explicit N/A path to PF-TSK-022 Lightweight Path L4 'Assess Test Coverage' for refactorings whose target IS test code (assertion thresholds, fixture data). Currently L4 asks coverage questions that don't apply when the modified test verifies its own behavior. One-line clarification suffices.
Notes: PF-FEE-1053 (TD249, 4-line numeric tolerance change in test_large_projects.py): L4 was structurally pro-forma. Test-only shortcut at L8 already acknowledges this case for documentation; L4 needs parallel acknowledgment.

Description: Tighten PF-TSK-085 Step 2 audit gate language. Currently says 'Audit Status is empty or ⬜ Not Audited' must pass audit first. Change to 'Audit Status must be ✅ Audit Approved. Any other value (empty, ⬜ Not Audited, 🔄 Needs Update, 🔴 Audit Failed) blocks baseline capture' so non-Approved states are unambiguously blocking.
Notes: PF-FEE-1058: PH-007/PH-008 had 🔄 Needs Update referencing the prior audit (TE-TAR-070), while the current re-audit (TE-TAR-071) was ✅ Audit Approved. Strict reading didn't trigger the gate; surfaced via tracking-vs-source-of-truth verification discipline. One-line edit.

Description: Add 'Tracking Consistency' sub-check to PF-TSK-030 Performance Test Criterion 3 (Baseline Readiness): verify performance-test-tracking.md Tolerance column matches code assertions. Drift here indicates upstream refactoring left documentation behind. Would have caught the PD-REF-220 drift during the audit proper rather than at finalization.
Notes: PF-FEE-1057: TE-TAR-071 re-audit caught Tolerance-column drift only at finalization (Minor Fix Authority). Sub-check during audit's Baseline Readiness criterion would surface earlier. Cross-task: PF-TSK-022 should also update tracking files when refactoring touches mirrored values.

Description: Update PF-TSK-026 lightweight-path callout in Step 5 to explicitly note interaction with Step 4.5 (Pilot Decision) and Step 21 conditional archive. Currently the callout focuses only on which steps to skip; the pilot-related conditionals are independent of the lightweight-path collapse. Add: 'If a pilot is chosen at Step 4.5, Step 21 archive is still deferred per the conditional, even on lightweight extensions.'
Notes: PF-FEE-1065 (PF-IMP-686/PF-PRO-030 implementation): the pilot infrastructure was added in this session, but the lightweight-path callout doesn't reference the new Step 4.5/Step 21 conditional. Worth a one-sentence update to keep the callout current.

Description: Add sub-bullet to PF-TSK-010 Step 12 deduplication guidance: also search technical-debt-tracking.md alongside process-improvement-tracking.md. Recurring script defects often land in tech debt first when discovered mid-task; current task references only process-improvement-tracking.md.
Notes: PF-FEE-986 (Tools Review session 2026-04-22): TD221/222/225/230 all covered the same New-IntegrationNarrative.ps1 issue — caught only because the agent independently checked tech debt. Codifying the rule would make the dedup mechanical.

Description: Clarify PF-TSK-022 Lightweight Path one-Item-vs-multi-Item ambiguity. When a single TD has multiple sub-findings, the path doc currently implies one Item per TD ('copy the Item N section for each additional debt item'). Add explicit guidance: 'Single TD with multiple sub-findings = 1 Item with multiple Changes Made bullets; multiple TDs batched in one session = multiple Items.'
Notes: PF-FEE-1054 (TD252 with 4 sub-findings under one TD ID): both interpretations work; explicit guidance removes ambiguity for future sessions. Single occurrence but trivially-cheap clarification.

Description: Document multi-metric sub-ID convention in performance-testing-guide.md. Convention `<TestID>-<metric>` (e.g., PH-001-scan, PH-001-move, PH-005-total, PH-005-avg, PH-007-net, PH-007-op-delta) is the established pattern but lives only in the performance_db.py data — undocumented anywhere. Add a section noting the pattern and that the primary asserted metric is the canonical baseline.
Notes: PF-FEE-1058 (PH-007/PH-008 baseline capture): had to discover the convention via `export | head`. Cross-references with potential feedback_db.py `list` subcommand IMP.
